headline: Homeless Population Health Needs Unmet as Street Count Reaches 8,400
context: JKM's 2025 census counted 8,400 homeless individuals with most having untreated chronic health conditions.

cards.big: 8,400 homeless people counted in 2025 with 72% having at least one untreated health condition
cards.sub: Mental illness substance use and chronic disease are most common unmet needs

cards.big: Only 3 cities operate dedicated healthcare outreach programs for homeless populations
cards.sub: KL Penang and JB have NGO-supported mobile clinics while other cities have nothing

cards.big: Homeless individuals use emergency departments 5 times more than housed populations
cards.sub: Emergency care for preventable conditions costs RM28 million annually

cards.big: Houston Texas reduced chronic homelessness 63% through housing-first with integrated health services
cards.sub: Combining shelter with healthcare proved more cost-effective than emergency-only approach

cards.big: Healthcare for the homeless is not charity—it is cheaper than letting them use emergency rooms
Should every city with over 100 homeless residents have a dedicated health outreach team
